title: Lathyrus angustifolius
taxonomy: Lathyrus angustifolius is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is classified in the subgenus Lathyrus, which includes other annual or biennial species. Lathyrus angustifolius is further classified into the genus Lathyrus, which contains about 160 species of flowering plants. The exact classification of Lathyrus angustifolius can be found within the larger family of Fabaceae.
uses: Lathyrus angustifolius has been reported to have various medicinal uses, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's seeds are said to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making them useful for wound healing and skin infections. In traditional medicine, the plant's leaves and stems are used to treat digestive issues and other ailments. Additionally, Lathyrus angustifolius is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
benefits: Lathyrus angustifolius, also known as narrow-leaved peashooter, contains isoflavones and other compounds that may have potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, the toxicity of the plant's seeds, particularly the toxin lathyrine, can be a concern for human consumption. Consuming large amounts of the seeds over a long period may cause neurological damage and other health issues. In moderation, the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems may be used in herbal teas and infusions. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential risks of Lathyrus angustifolius.
properties: Lathyrus angustifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved pea, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its therapeutic properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Its leaves and flowers are used to make infusions and decoctions, which are believed to possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. Additionally, Lathyrus angustifolius is thought to have diuretic and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues and urinary problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lathyrus angustifolius as a medicinal plant.
constituents: Lathyrus angustifolius, commonly known as narrow-leaved peavine or narrow-leaved pea, is a perennial legume. It contains various active constituents, including alkaloids such as lathyrine, which have been reported to exhibit neurotoxic and cytotoxic properties. Lathyrine and other alkaloids present in this plant have been shown to have potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Additionally, the plant's constituents may also possess antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of Lathyrus angustifolius' active constituents on human health.
parts: Lathyrus angustifolius, commonly known as purple pea, has been reported to have various medicinal properties. The plant's seeds, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The seeds contain a toxin called ODAP, which can be toxic in large quantities, but in small amounts, they are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. However, the exact medicinal parts and their uses require further research and confirmation. More studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Lathyrus angustifolius.
preparations: Lathyrus angustifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved pea or purple pea, is used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The plant's preparations are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive problems. Herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures are commonly made from the plant's leaves, stems, and flowers. These preparations may also be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.
side_effects: Lathyrus angustifolius, a plant commonly known as narrow-leaf pea or Italian pea, has been found to contain a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid) in its seeds. While generally considered safe, consuming large quantities of the seeds may lead to adverse health effects. Prolonged consumption of the seeds has been linked to neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness, paralysis, and other neurological symptoms. The severity of these side effects is often dependent on the duration and quantity of Lathyrus angustifolius seed consumption. However, the exact mechanisms and potential long-term effects of ODAP exposure are still being studied.
intro: Lathyrus angustifolius, also known as narrow-leaved pea or narrow-leaved vetchling, is a perennial legume native to Europe. The plant has been reported to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as arthritis and cardiovascular conditions. Bioactive constituents of Lathyrus angustifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lathyrus angustifolius is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems.
